chapter_title.en: Chapter on Establishing Ihram, Its Conditions, Nullification, and the Prayer for it

text: رِوَايَةِ عَبْدِ اَلرَّحْمَنِ بْنِ اَلْحَجَّاجِ عَنْهُ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : أَنَّهُ صَلَّى رَكْعَتَيْنِ وَ عَقَدَ فِي مَسْجِدِ اَلشَّجَرَةِ ثُمَّ خَرَجَ فَأُتِيَ بِخَبِيصٍ فِيهِ زَعْفَرَانٌ فَأَكَلَ قَبْلَ أَنْ يُلَبِّيَ مِنْهُ.

en.bab-ul-qaim-publications:
Hadith.2567 - In a narration from Abdul Rahman ibn al-Hajjaj, from Imam (as):
He prayed two units of prayer and entered Ihram at Masjid al-Shajarah. Then he went out and was brought a dessert containing saffron, and he ate from it before reciting the Talbiyah.
